senses.definition: to maximize one's current advantage or position of strength in a particular situation

examples.x: After winning the first two sets, the tennis player pressed home her advantage by aggressively attacking her opponent's weaknesses.
In the business world, it's important to press home your advantage when you have a competitive edge in the market.
During negotiations, he knew it was essential to press home his advantage by securing favorable terms for his company.
The team decided to press home their advantage by launching a new advertising campaign while their competitors were struggling.
She realized that this was the perfect opportunity to press home her advantage and expand her customer base.

senses.definition: to take advantage of or become involved in an activity that someone else has started in an attempt to achieve the same amount of success or profit

examples.x: When they saw the new restaurant opening, several other chefs wanted to get in on the act and open their own places nearby.
After the initial investors saw the startup's success, many others wanted to be in on the act and provide funding.
She noticed her friends having so much fun volunteering at the community event that she decided to get in on the act.
As soon as the fashion trend took off, many designers were eager to be in on the act and create their own versions.
The team had an innovative approach, and competitors were quick to get in on the act by adopting similar strategies.

senses.definition: to make effort to turn a difficult situation in one's favor

examples.x: During the company's financial crisis, some competitors tried to fish in troubled waters by poaching our clients.
Political opportunists often fish in troubled waters, exploiting crises for their own gain.
He saw the disagreement as a chance to fish in troubled waters and advance his own agenda.
The detective knew that criminals often fish in troubled waters, taking advantage of chaotic situations to commit crimes.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv wˈʌnz kˈeɪk ænd ˈiːt ɪt tˈuː ɔːɹ

senses.definition: to desire to have two things that are impossible to have without sacrificing the other

word_list.word: [know] which side {one's} bread is buttered on
word_list.pronunciation: nˈoʊ wˌɪtʃ sˈaɪd wˈʌnz bɹˈɛd ɪz bˈʌɾɚd ˈɑːn

senses.definition: to know the exact way to treat others or how to act so as to get what one desires

examples.x: She knows which side her bread is buttered on, so she always supports the boss's ideas.
He's a smart businessman; he knows which side his bread is buttered on.
It's important to know which side your bread is buttered on and make decisions that advance your career.
She married into a wealthy family, and she knows which side her bread is buttered on.

senses.definition: to benefit from two opposing or contradictory situations or options at the same time

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ɡˈɪv ˌɛsbˈiː ɐn ˈiːvən bɹˈeɪk

senses.definition: to take advantage of someone who is easily tricked instead of giving them a fair chance

examples.x: Despite his hard work, he felt that the boss did not give him an even break when it came to promotions.
She was frustrated because her opponent in the game did not give her an even break.

senses.definition: to treat someone in such a kind and generous manner that they become greedy and ungrateful to one

examples.x: I let my coworker borrow my pen for a moment, and now they keep asking for more office supplies—give them an inch, and they'll ask for the whole stationery cabinet.
The student was allowed to submit their assignment a day late, and now they're requesting additional extensions—give them an inch, and they'll seek more leniency.
The team leader gave a team member some flexibility in their work schedule, and now they're pushing for even more flexible hours—give them an inch, and they'll seek greater flexibility.

The company made a small adjustment to their product based on customer feedback, and now customers are requesting extensive customization options—give them an inch, and they'll desire significant alterations.
The parent allowed their child to have dessert before dinner as a one-time treat, and now the child expects it every day—give them an inch, and they'll develop heightened dessert expectations.

senses.definition: used to ask if someone wants extra or additional benefits or features, often in a playful manner

examples.x: The basic package includes internet and cable TV, but if you want jam on it, we can add premium channels at a discounted rate.
The offer includes a laptop and a smartphone, but if you want jam on it, we can provide a top-of-the-line tablet as well.
